mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which drives corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO growth on 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in less than 50% of periods with INDPRO rising above 102.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods after UNRATE exceeds 5%, the proportion of positive 20-day returns is greater than 50% or the average 20-day return is significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized weakening of consumer demand and residential investment, which depresses aggregate demand and corporate revenues, resulting in negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05 one-sided).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, rising consumer prices (CPI) prompt further tightening expectations that compress equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns. The negative effect of inflation on equities is conditional on the restrictive monetary policy stance.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPIAUCSL is rising month-over-month, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the rank correlation between CPI change and forward returns is not negative (Spearman rho >= 0.0).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 index level indicates a contraction in manufacturing output, which signals recessionary conditions and results in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: After INDPRO drops below 100, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns is greater than 40% or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market conditions that support consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 or not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and retail sales decline concurrently, it signals a synchronized contraction in supply and demand that typically precedes recessions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In periods when both INDPRO and RSAFS are month-over-month declining, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05 one-sided).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate of more than 25 basis points in a month represents an unexpected monetary tightening that negatively impacts equity market liquidity and valuation, causing short-term negative returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: After a month with a FEDFUNDS increase greater than 0.25%, the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return over 5 days is not significantly negative (p > 0.05 one-sided).
